title: Effect of polymer viscosity on physicochemical properties and ocular tolerance of FB-loaded PLGA nanospheres

[EXPERIMENTAL_DESIGN_BLOCK]
Experimental design: varying concentrations or batches. Response surface plot of the effect of the interaction between FB concentration and pH (cFB:pH) on the mean particle size (cP188 = 15 mg/mL; cPLGA = 9 mg/mL), mean size (nm) = 545.57 − 107.04 pH − 184.84 cFB + 7.91 pH 2 + 28.54 cFB 2 + 24.75 pH:cFB. Download: Download full-size image Fig. 3 .

[OPTIMIZATION_RESULT_BLOCK]
Optimization result: best/highest/efficiency/loading outcome. Mean particle size, zeta potential and entrapment efficiency of FB-loaded nanospheres produced with low viscosity PLGA in comparison to nanospheres produced with high viscosity PLGA [24] .

[PARAGRAPH_BLOCK]
Mean particle size and zeta potential of nanospheres produced with PLGA of low (0.32–0.44 dL/g) and high viscosity (0.7–1.1 dL/g), monitored for 75 days. Download: Download full-size image Fig. 7 . Transmission (T) and back scattering (BS) profiles of nanospheres produced with PLGA of low (0.32–0.44 dL/g) and high viscosity (0.7–1.1 dL/g), as a function of time (upper: 0–24 h) and of sample height (lower: 0–65 mm).
